expectations.text: Response grades the expectation as FAILED (not passed) -- the executor used 'lat'/'lon' not 'latitude'/'longitude', used hourly not current, and used temperature_2m in hourly context not current context
expectations.evidence: Response says 'Decision: FAIL' and lists: '1. It used lat and lon instead of the required latitude and longitude parameter names. 2. It used the hourly=temperature_2m parameter instead of the required current=temperature_2m,wind_speed_10m parameter.' Correct grade with correct reasoning.

expectations.text: Response writes a grading.json entry with the exact fields: text, passed, evidence
expectations.evidence: grading.json entry shows: '"text": "Response provides the exact endpoint GET..."', '"passed": false', '"evidence": "Response provided..."'. All three required field names present and correctly used.

expectations.text: Response quotes verbatim from the mock executor response as the evidence string (not a paraphrase)
expectations.evidence: Evidence field contains verbatim quote: 'Response provided "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?lat=\"\+lat+\"&lon=\"\+lon+\"&hourly=temperature_2m" which uses incorrect parameter names...' -- actual text from the mock response is quoted.

expectations.text: Response identifies at least two specific deviations: the parameter name difference (lat vs latitude) AND the hourly vs current context difference
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly numbers both: '1. It used lat and lon instead of the required latitude and longitude parameter names.' and '2. It used the hourly=temperature_2m parameter instead of the required current=temperature_2m,wind_speed_10m parameter.' Both deviations named.
